purpose: Add configurable Agent/Worker strategy modes to Ananta so different execution styles can be selected explicitly: OpenCode-like interactive agent loop, OpenAI Codex CLI-like patch/command agent, Hermes-like proposal/review agent, Ananta-native deterministic/tool-first worker, and generic OpenAI-compatible LLM modes. Each mode must have its own safe default strategy chain and fallback behavior.
motivation: Different coding agents behave differently. OpenCode-like flows are usually iterative tool loops; Codex CLI-like flows may operate more patch/command oriented; Hermes-like flows may be proposal/review first; Ananta-native should be deterministic, auditable and policy-first.
Ananta should not force every provider into one generic JSON-only flow.
Users should be able to configure which mode a worker/agent uses for a task kind, project, role or provider.
Fallbacks should be explicit and safe, not accidental: native tool-calling before JSON fallback, JSON before flexible normalization, human review before unsafe execution.

desired_modes.description: Interactive coding-agent loop. Prefer iterative tool use: inspect files, edit files, run tests, observe output, continue. Best for larger coding tasks where multiple steps are needed.
default_safety: shell execution denied by default unless task policy enables it
tool budget and loop budget required

desired_modes.description: Patch/command oriented CLI-worker mode. Prefer structured patch/file changes and bounded verification. Good for code edits, tests and refactorings.
default_safety: no raw shell from prose without explicit allow_shell_execution

desired_modes.description: Proposal/review-first agent. Prefer plan/proposal/artifact review and avoid direct mutation unless explicitly enabled later.
default_safety: outputs are proposal/advisory unless mode explicitly allows apply
good for architecture review and task planning

desired_modes.description: Ananta-controlled deterministic/tool-first worker mode. Prefer explicit tools, deterministic handlers, artifact manifests and verification.
default_safety: artifact manifest or synthesized diff required

desired_modes.description: Generic OpenAI-compatible provider mode. Prefer native tool_calls if the provider/model supports them, then JSON schema fallback.
default_safety: json_schema_llm only requires JSON inside that fallback path

tasks.title: Introduce StrategyMode model and presets
tasks.problem: Current strategy selection is mostly policy/strategy-order based. It lacks explicit named modes that capture behavioral differences between OpenCode-like, Codex CLI-like, Hermes-like and Ananta-native workers.
implementation_steps: Add StrategyMode dataclass/Pydantic model with id, description, strategy_order, fallback_policy, execution_policy and completion_policy defaults.
Add presets for opencode_like, codex_cli_like, hermes_like, ananta_native and openai_compatible_tool_calling.
Add resolver that merges task/project/worker/role/task_kind/global config in deterministic order.
Expose effective_strategy_mode in proposal metadata for audit.
Do not remove existing ProposePolicy; StrategyMode should feed/build ProposePolicy and execution/completion policies.
acceptance_criteria: Each desired mode resolves to an explicit strategy order and fallback policy.
new_software_project can select opencode_like or openai_compatible_tool_calling without changing code.
hermes_like resolves to proposal/review mode and does not execute mutations by default.
ananta_native resolves to deterministic/tool-first and artifact-first completion.
Tests cover config precedence and defaults.

tasks.title: Add agent-loop strategy for OpenCode-like mode
tasks.problem: OpenCode-like behavior is not just one LLM response. It is an iterative loop: inspect -> edit -> run -> observe -> continue. Ananta needs a controlled mode for that instead of forcing everything through one-shot JSON/tool_calls.
implementation_steps: Implement bounded loop: model receives state + tool results, returns next tool_call(s), Ananta validates/executes, result goes back into context.
Add loop budget: max iterations, max tool calls, max runtime, max file writes, max command risk.
Prefer file tools over shell commands. Shell requires allow_shell_execution=true and command risk approval.
Collect artifacts after loop exits.
Stop conditions: completed_by_verification, no_executable_tool_call, policy_denied, max_iterations, human_review_required.
The loop must never call tools directly from the LLM. Ananta remains the executor and policy gate.
acceptance_criteria: OpenCode-like mode can complete a small coding task through multiple tool iterations.
Tool results are fed back into the next model call.
Loop stops on budget exhaustion and returns needs_review, not infinite retry.
Shell from model text remains denied unless policy explicitly allows it.
Artifacts and verification decide completion.

tasks.title: Add Codex-CLI-like patch/command strategy mode
tasks.problem: Some agents behave more like patch-producing CLI workers. Ananta should support a mode that prefers patches/file changes plus verification instead of free-form agent loops.
implementation_steps: Accept structured patch proposals or file-change proposals from a CLI/provider adapter.
Validate paths and patch applicability inside workspace.
Apply patch only through controlled patch service.
Run verification after apply.
Fallback to human_review when patch is ambiguous or does not apply cleanly.
Avoid direct shell execution unless command policy explicitly allows it.
acceptance_criteria: Patch-only output can become PatchProposalArtifact, not direct shell execution.
Valid patch applies inside workspace and produces artifacts.
Invalid patch returns needs_review or retryable_failed.
Verification result controls completion.

tasks.title: Add Hermes-like proposal/review strategy mode
tasks.problem: Hermes-like behavior should be proposal/review-first and not silently mutate the workspace. This needs explicit mode semantics so Hermes can be used safely alongside mutating workers.
implementation_steps: Implement hermes_like mode as non-mutating by default.
Return PlannerProposalArtifact, PatchProposalArtifact or AdvisoryProposalArtifact, not direct execute mutation.
Require explicit apply/approval policy before Hermes proposals can affect workspace.
Document Hermes phase boundaries: proposal/review now, optional apply later.
acceptance_criteria: Hermes-like mode cannot execute write_file/shell directly by default.
Hermes-like mode produces auditable proposal artifacts.
Attempted execute mutation in Hermes-like mode is denied or needs_review unless explicitly enabled.

tasks.title: Make fallback mechanisms configurable per mode
tasks.problem: Fallbacks must be intentional. For example, new_project may require an LLM and refuse deterministic fallback, while ananta_native might allow deterministic handlers first. Hermes-like should fall back to human review, not mutation.
implementation_steps: Add fallback flags: allow_json_schema_fallback, allow_flexible_normalization, allow_worker_fallback, allow_deterministic_fallback, allow_human_review, llm_required.
Orchestrator must respect disabled fallback classes and record skipped strategies with reason disabled_by_strategy_mode.
If llm_required=true and all LLM strategies are unavailable, stop with llm_required_but_unavailable instead of deterministic fallback.
If allow_flexible_normalization=false, malformed model prose goes to human_review/needs_review.
If allow_deterministic_fallback=false, deterministic_handler is skipped even if present in generic chain.
acceptance_criteria: Fallback behavior is deterministic and visible in attempted_strategies metadata.
opencode_like can use JSON/flexible fallback but shell remains policy-gated.
hermes_like does not fallback into mutation strategies.
new_software_project can be configured to require LLM and block deterministic fallback.
Tests cover disabled/enabled fallback combinations.

tasks.title: Expose strategy mode configuration in API/UI
tasks.problem: The user needs to choose which worker runs which task and in which mode. Strategy modes should be configurable, visible and auditable in the frontend/API.
implementation_steps: Add strategy_mode field to agent/worker config and optional task override.
Expose effective_strategy_mode in task propose response and task detail API.
UI should allow selecting mode from presets and optionally advanced fallback flags.
Store mode config as structured JSON but validate against known preset/schema.
Show attempted_strategies, selected_strategy and skipped_by_mode in task details.
acceptance_criteria: User can configure an agent as opencode_like/codex_cli_like/hermes_like/ananta_native/openai_compatible_tool_calling.
Task override can select a different mode for one task.
Effective mode and fallback decisions are visible in API/UI.
Invalid mode config is rejected with clear error.

tasks.title: Document mode semantics and provider mapping
tasks.problem: Future maintainers need clear docs explaining that different providers/agents are not forced into one behavior model.
implementation_steps: Document each strategy mode with when to use it, default strategy order, fallback behavior and safety limits.
Explain native tool-calling vs JSON schema fallback vs flexible normalization.
Explain OpenCode-like loop vs Codex-CLI-like patch flow vs Hermes proposal/review flow.
Add sequence diagrams for opencode_like and ananta_native flows.
Document that Ananta executes tools, not the LLM/provider directly.
acceptance_criteria: Docs clearly state that JSON-only output is not required globally.
Docs clearly state shell from model text is denied by default.
Docs explain safe fallback behavior per mode.

global_done_definition: Ananta has named, configurable strategy modes for opencode_like, codex_cli_like, hermes_like, ananta_native and openai_compatible_tool_calling.
Each mode resolves into explicit ProposePolicy, execution policy, fallback policy and completion policy.
Fallbacks are configurable and audited in attempted_strategies metadata.
OpenCode-like mode supports bounded iterative tool loops.
Codex-CLI-like mode supports controlled patch/file-change flows.
Hermes-like mode is non-mutating by default.
Tool-calling remains preferred where supported; JSON schema is fallback; flexible normalization is policy-gated recovery.
API/UI exposes effective mode and selected strategy.

summary_bottom.assessment: This TODO generalizes the current Autopilot strategy work into configurable worker modes. It avoids forcing all providers into JSON-only behavior and allows Ananta to support OpenCode-like, Codex-CLI-like, Hermes-like and Ananta-native flows safely side-by-side.
summary_bottom.most_important_next_step: Implement WSM-T001 and WSM-T005 first: named strategy modes plus explicit fallback policy.
